data.name: MBIX
data.notes:
Not-for-profit internet exchange. Member-oriented and flexible.

Secure route filtering using IRR data and RPKI ROA.

For prefixes to pass validation peers must maintain IRR route objects, or RPKI ROAs, or ARIN WHOIS OriginAS entries. Peers with downstreams must also maintain IRR as-set objects, and place the as-set name in the peeringdb.com "IRR Record" field. The downstreams must also maintain objects.

data.name: IX.br (PTT.br) Recife
data.notes:
This is the Peering information for the IX.br (PTT.br) Route Servers.

There are two route servers per IXP for redundancy purposes in Recife

For IX.br (PTT.br) Recife we recommend a maximum-prefixes setting 40K for IPv4 and 20K for IPv6

Communities added by the route server  to identify the location where the prefix was received:
* 16-bit ASN: 26162:65081
* 32-bit ASN: RT:26162:65081 or RO:26162:65081


IX.br Communities BGP Policy

http://docs.ix.br/doc/politica-de-tratamento-de-communities-no-ix-br-v4_3-english.pdf

data.name: IX.br (PTT.br) Manaus
data.notes:
This is the Peering information for the IX.br (PTT.br) Route Servers.

There are two route servers per IXP for redundancy purposes in Manaus

For IX.br (PTT.br) Manaus we recommend a maximum-prefixes setting 2K for IPv4 and 1K for IPv6

The following communities are implemented:

Do not advertise the prefix for ASNxx:
* 16-bit ASN: 65000:ASNxx
* 32-bit ASN: RT: 65000: ASNxx or RO:65000:ASNxx

Announce the prefix for ASNxx only:
* 16-bit ASN: 65001:ASNxx
* 32-bit ASN: RT:65001:ASNxx or RO:65001:ASNxx

Add one prepend in the AS-Path of the prefixes sent to the ASNxx
* 16-bit ASN use the communitie 64601: ASNxx
* 32-bit ASN use the communitie RT:64601:ASNxx or RO:64601:ASNxx
  
Add two prepends in the AS-Path of the prefixes sent to the ASNxx
* 16-bit ASN: 64602:ASNxx
* 32-bit ASN: RT:64602:ASNxx or RO:64602:ASNxx

Add three prepends in the AS-Path of the prefixes sent to the ASNxx
* 16-bit ASN: 64603:ASNxx
* 32-bit ASN: RT:64603:ASNxx or RO:64603:ASNxx

Communitie to identify the ASN that sent the prefix to the route server:
* 16-bit ASN: 26162:SOURCE_ASN
* 32-bit ASN: RT:26162:SOURCE_ASN or 
* 32-bit ASN: RO:26162:SOURCE_ASN

Communities added by the route server  to identify the location where the prefix was received:
* 16-bit ASN: 26162:65092
* 32-bit ASN: RT:26162:65092 or RO:26162:65092

data.name: MASS-IX
data.notes:
### Primary Service Area:

Boston, MA metro and Massachusetts

### Ports:

First year is currently free on a two-year commitment. Up to two 10G or 100G ports.

+ 10GE:  $100 MRC, $0 NRC
+ 100GE:  $300 MRC, $0 NRC
+ 400GE:  $1,000 MRC, $5,000 NRC

Customers of [TOWARDEX](https://www.towardex.com) who are currently not connected to MASS-IX may request a free connection, _subject to facilities and availability_.

data.name: SFO-IX
data.notes:
SFO-IX project:

Please see the linked website for details on how to influence its prefix-filtering policies when peering with it. The two route servers run under a Alcatel-Lucent SR7750-12e and participants are advised to peer with both for redundancy.

The route servers do not prepend own ASN from the AS_path by default.

We recommend a maximum-prefixes setting of 200k for IPv4 and 40k for IPv6 for AS13094.

data.name: YXEIX
data.notes:
YXEIX Route Servers offer secure route filtering using IRR data and RPKI ROA.

For prefixes to pass validation on the YXEIX route servers, peers must maintain IRR route objects, RPKI ROAs, and/or ARIN WHOIS OriginAS entries. 

Peers with downstream networks must also maintain IRR as-set objects, and place the as-set name in the peeringdb.com "IRR Record" field.

Please contact the YXEIX (Info@YXEIX.com) if you require assistance or would like more details about our filtering platform. 

Happy Peering!

![YXEIX][logo]

[logo]: https://yxeix.ca/img/YXEIX_Logo.png "Saskatoon Internet Exchange"

Available Now at QTS


AS 5118 is used for Route Server for Public Peering LAN . Please also peer with the route viewer /looking glass AS65000 on .1 / ::1. Peering with AS5118 will include all participants connected to the route servers plus ROOT Servers and AS112.

data.name: Eurasia Peering IX
data.notes:
Eurasia Peering IX is a fast-growing peering community designed for low latency traffic exchange between clouds, data centres, networks, enterprises, etc.

1/10/25/40/100Gb ports are available.

Eurasia Peering IX supports both Public and Private Peering.

For Route Server info please visit www.peeringdb.com/net/15436. 

To join us please e-mail to sales@eurasiapeering.ru.

For IX.br (PTT.br) Teresina we recommend a maximum-prefixes setting 10K for IPv4 and 5K for IPv6

Communities added by the route server  to identify the location where the prefix was received:
* 16-bit ASN: 26162:65086
* 32-bit ASN: RT:26162:65086 or RO:26162:65086


IX.br Communities BGP Policy

http://docs.ix.br/doc/politica-de-tratamento-de-communities-no-ix-br-v4_3-english.pdf


Communities BGP Table

http://docs.ix.br/doc/communities-table-ix-br-v2_1-14082024.pdf

data.name: MMIX Yangon
data.notes: MMIX is the first IXP in Myanmar.  MMIX is neutral and industrial owned exchange.  It was founded on September, 2017.  Our POP is at MICT Park, Hlaing township, Yangon, Myanmar.

data.name: CFLIX (Central Florida Internet Exchange)
data.notes: CFLIX will be merged into Any2Miami in Mid-November.
